articles.headline: India Unveils Phase II of National Green Hydrogen Mission, Targets Enhanced Production and Export
articles.explainer:
• The Union Cabinet approved the second phase of the National Green Hydrogen Mission, aiming to significantly scale up indigenous production capacity.
• Phase II focuses on developing green hydrogen export hubs, establishing a robust supply chain, and attracting global investments.
• Key incentives include Production Linked Incentives (PLI) for electrolyser manufacturing and green hydrogen production, along with R&D support.
• The mission targets a substantial reduction in fossil fuel imports and an acceleration towards India's net-zero emissions goal by 2070.
• Emphasis is placed on creating a skilled workforce and fostering innovation in green hydrogen technologies.
gsSubTopics: Energy
Infrastructure
Environmental pollution and degradation
Conservation
Science and Technology-developments and their applications and effects in everyday life
Indigenization of technology
articles.source: Ministry of New and Renewable Energy
prelimsPoints: National Green Hydrogen Mission launched in 2023.
Phase II focuses on scaling up production and export.
Key components include SIGHT (Strategic Interventions for Green Hydrogen Transition) Programme.
Nodal Ministry: Ministry of New and Renewable Energy (MNRE).
Targets include 5 MMT (Million Metric Tonnes) green hydrogen production capacity by 2030.
mainsPoints: Discuss the economic potential of green hydrogen for India, including job creation and export opportunities.
Analyze the environmental benefits and its role in achieving India's climate commitments.
Examine the technological challenges and R&D requirements for large-scale green hydrogen production and storage.
Evaluate the geopolitical implications of India becoming a major green hydrogen producer and exporter.
Critically assess the policy incentives and regulatory framework needed for successful mission implementation.
articles.imageDescription: Illustration of a green hydrogen production facility with solar panels and electrolysers, symbolizing clean energy.
articles.trivia: Hydrogen is the most abundant chemical substance in the universe, making up roughly 75% of all baryonic mass.

articles.headline: Supreme Court Upholds Key Provisions of Digital Personal Data Protection Act, 2023
articles.explainer:
• The Supreme Court, in a landmark judgment, largely upheld the constitutional validity of the Digital Personal Data Protection Act (DPDPA), 2023.
• The ruling addressed petitions challenging aspects related to data processing, consent mechanisms, and the powers of the Data Protection Board.
• The Court emphasized the balance between individual privacy rights and legitimate state interests, including national security and public order.
• Specific clarifications were issued regarding the 'deemed consent' provisions and the independence of the Data Protection Board.
• This judgment provides much-needed legal clarity for technology companies and data fiduciaries operating in India.
gsSubTopics: Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation
Structure, organization and functioning of the Executive and the Judiciary
Fundamental rights
prelimsPoints: DPDPA, 2023 replaced the Personal Data Protection Bill, 2019.
Key principles include consent, data minimization, accuracy, storage limitation.
Establishes the Data Protection Board of India.
Applies to processing of digital personal data within India and certain offshore processing.
Right to be forgotten and right to correction/erasure are part of the Act.
mainsPoints: Analyze how the DPDPA balances individual privacy rights with national security and economic interests.
Discuss the challenges in implementing the DPDPA, particularly for small and medium enterprises (SMEs).
Evaluate the role and independence of the Data Protection Board in safeguarding citizen's data.
Compare India's data protection framework with international standards like GDPR.
Examine the implications of the Supreme Court's judgment for the digital economy and innovation in India.
articles.imageDescription: A gavel and a digital screen displaying code, symbolizing law and technology.
articles.trivia: The 'Right to Privacy' was declared a fundamental right by the Supreme Court of India in the K.S. Puttaswamy judgment of 2017.

articles.headline: India Hosts BIMSTEC Ministerial Meeting, Emphasizes Regional Connectivity and Trade Facilitation
articles.explainer:
• India hosted the annual Ministerial Meeting of the Bay of Bengal Initiative for Multi-Sectoral Technical and Economic Cooperation (BIMSTEC).
• The agenda focused on accelerating regional connectivity projects, boosting intra-BIMSTEC trade, and strengthening cooperation in disaster management and climate resilience.
• Member states reaffirmed their commitment to the BIMSTEC Master Plan for Transport Connectivity, aiming to enhance physical and digital linkages.
• Discussions also covered food security, public health cooperation, and combating transnational crimes.
• The meeting concluded with a joint declaration emphasizing a 'prosperous, resilient, and peaceful Bay of Bengal region'.
gsSubTopics: Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests
prelimsPoints: BIMSTEC comprises 7 member states: Bangladesh, Bhutan, India, Myanmar, Nepal, Sri Lanka, and Thailand.
Formed in 1997 with the Bangkok Declaration.
Focuses on 14 priority sectors, including trade, technology, energy, transport, tourism, and fisheries.
Secretariat located in Dhaka, Bangladesh.
India considers BIMSTEC a key platform for its 'Act East' and 'Neighborhood First' policies.
mainsPoints: Analyze the strategic importance of BIMSTEC for India's regional foreign policy and economic interests.
Discuss the challenges hindering BIMSTEC's progress in achieving its objectives, particularly compared to SAARC.
Evaluate the potential of enhanced connectivity projects (physical and digital) within the BIMSTEC region.
Examine how BIMSTEC can contribute to addressing non-traditional security threats in the Bay of Bengal.
Suggest measures to strengthen BIMSTEC's institutional framework and enhance its effectiveness.
articles.imageDescription: Flags of BIMSTEC member states arranged around a map of the Bay of Bengal region.
articles.trivia: Four of the BIMSTEC member states (India, Bangladesh, Myanmar, Sri Lanka) are also members of SAARC.

articles.headline: RBI Releases Biannual Financial Stability Report, Cautions on Global Headwinds
articles.explainer:
• The Reserve Bank of India (RBI) released its latest Financial Stability Report (FSR), assessing the health of India's financial system.
• The report noted resilience in the banking sector, with improved asset quality and capital adequacy ratios.
• However, it cautioned about potential risks stemming from global economic slowdown, geopolitical tensions, and persistent inflation pressures.
• Non-banking financial companies (NBFCs) also showed signs of stability, though specific segments require close monitoring.
• The RBI emphasized the need for continued vigilance and proactive policy measures to mitigate emerging risks.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment
Government Budgeting
Monetary policy
prelimsPoints: FSR is published biannually by RBI.
Assesses the risks to financial stability and resilience of the Indian financial system.
Covers commercial banks, urban cooperative banks, NBFCs, and financial markets.
Key indicators include asset quality, capital adequacy, profitability, and liquidity.
RBI's mandate includes maintaining financial stability.
mainsPoints: Analyze the current state of India's banking and NBFC sectors as highlighted by the FSR.
Discuss the domestic and global factors posing risks to India's financial stability.
Evaluate the effectiveness of RBI's regulatory and supervisory measures in maintaining financial sector resilience.
Examine the interlinkages between the financial sector and the real economy, particularly concerning credit flow.
Suggest policy recommendations for strengthening financial stability in the face of evolving challenges.
articles.imageDescription: A graph showing upward trends in financial stability indicators with a background of the RBI logo.
articles.trivia: The Reserve Bank of India was established on April 1, 1935, based on the recommendations of the Hilton Young Commission.

articles.headline: Project Tiger Completes 52 Years: Successes, Challenges, and Future Roadmap for Conservation
articles.explainer:
• Project Tiger, India's flagship tiger conservation program, marked its 52nd anniversary, celebrating significant strides in increasing tiger populations.
• A comprehensive review highlighted the expansion of protected areas, anti-poaching efforts, and community involvement as key successes.
• Challenges persist, including human-wildlife conflict, habitat fragmentation, and the impact of climate change on tiger corridors.
• The Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change outlined a future roadmap focusing on genetic diversity, smart patrolling, and landscape-level conservation.
• Emphasis was laid on integrating local communities into conservation strategies through livelihood support and awareness programs.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment
Biodiversity
Wildlife Protection Act
articles.source: Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change
prelimsPoints: Project Tiger launched in 1973 in India.
Administered by the National Tiger Conservation Authority (NTCA).
India is home to over 70% of the world's wild tiger population.
M-STrIPES (Monitoring System for Tigers – Intensive Protection and Ecological Status) is a key surveillance tool.
Total number of tiger reserves in India is 54 (as of 2024).
mainsPoints: Analyze the factors contributing to the success of Project Tiger in increasing tiger populations in India.
Discuss the major challenges facing tiger conservation efforts today, including human-wildlife conflict and habitat loss.
Evaluate the role of community participation and eco-tourism in sustainable tiger conservation.
Examine the impact of climate change on tiger habitats and corridors, and adaptation strategies.
Suggest innovative approaches for strengthening anti-poaching measures and ensuring genetic diversity of tigers.
articles.imageDescription: A majestic Bengal tiger walking in its natural habitat, symbolizing conservation success.
articles.trivia: Ranthambore National Park in Rajasthan was one of the first nine tiger reserves designated under Project Tiger in 1973.

articles.headline: ISRO Successfully Launches Advanced Earth Observation Satellite 'EOS-07'
articles.explainer:
• The Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) successfully launched the Earth Observation Satellite-07 (EOS-07) from Satish Dhawan Space Centre.
• EOS-07 is an advanced remote sensing satellite designed for high-resolution imaging and data collection for various applications.
• Its payloads include enhanced optical sensors and synthetic aperture radar (SAR) for all-weather, all-time imaging capabilities.
• The satellite will primarily support applications in agriculture, forestry, disaster management, urban planning, and oceanography.
• This launch further strengthens India's indigenous capabilities in space-based earth observation and data analytics.
gsSubTopics: Science and Technology-developments and their applications and effects in everyday life
Achievements of Indians in science & technology; indigenization of technology and developing new technology
prelimsPoints: EOS series satellites are designed for Earth observation.
EOS-07 uses advanced optical and SAR payloads.
PSLV (Polar Satellite Launch Vehicle) is ISRO's workhorse launch vehicle.
Applications include agriculture, disaster management, urban planning.
Satish Dhawan Space Centre (SDSC) in Sriharikota is India's primary spaceport.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of Earth Observation Satellites for India's socio-economic development and national security.
Analyze how advanced remote sensing technologies, like SAR, enhance disaster management and resource mapping capabilities.
Examine the role of ISRO in promoting self-reliance and innovation in space technology.
Evaluate the potential applications of geospatial data from EOS-07 in various sectors like smart cities and climate monitoring.
Discuss challenges and opportunities for India in the global space economy.
articles.imageDescription: An ISRO rocket launching into space, with the Earth visible in the background.
articles.trivia: ISRO was founded in 1969, succeeding the Indian National Committee for Space Research (INCOSPAR).

articles.headline: National Education Policy 2020 Implementation Review Highlights Progress in Skill Development
articles.explainer:
• A comprehensive review of the National Education Policy (NEP) 2020 implementation noted significant progress, particularly in vocational education and skill development.
• The Ministry of Education highlighted increased integration of vocational courses from secondary levels and the establishment of skill hubs.
• Challenges remain in achieving universal foundational literacy and numeracy, especially in remote areas.
• The review emphasized the need for greater teacher training, digital infrastructure enhancement, and equitable access to quality education.
• Stakeholders called for accelerated reforms to fully realize NEP's vision of a holistic and flexible education system.
gsSubTopics: Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors
prelimsPoints: NEP 2020 replaced the National Policy on Education of 1986.
Aims for universalization of education from pre-school to secondary level.
Introduces 5+3+3+4 curricular structure.
Focuses on multidisciplinary education, vocational integration, and critical thinking.
Establishes a National Research Foundation (NRF).
mainsPoints: Analyze the key achievements and persistent challenges in the implementation of NEP 2020.
Discuss the role of vocational education and skill development in addressing youth unemployment and enhancing employability.
Evaluate the effectiveness of digital learning initiatives in bridging educational disparities, especially post-pandemic.
Examine the importance of teacher training and capacity building for successful educational reforms.
Suggest policy measures to ensure equitable access to quality education across all sections of society, as envisioned by NEP.
articles.imageDescription: Students engaged in hands-on learning activities, symbolizing skill development and practical education.
articles.trivia: The Kothari Commission (1964-66) was instrumental in shaping India's education policy, leading to the first National Policy on Education in 1968.

prelimsSyllabus.subject: Society
prelimsSyllabus.topic: National Education Policy and Skill Development

articles.headline: India-US Joint Cyber Security Exercise 'Cyber Shield 2025' Concludes
articles.explainer:
• The joint India-US cyber security exercise, 'Cyber Shield 2025', successfully concluded, enhancing cooperation in combating cyber threats.
• The exercise simulated complex cyber-attack scenarios targeting critical infrastructure and government networks.
• Participants from both nations shared best practices in threat intelligence, incident response, and forensic analysis.
• The drill aimed to strengthen interoperability and build a coordinated response mechanism against state-sponsored and criminal cyber activities.
• This collaboration underscores the growing strategic partnership between India and the US in the digital domain.
gsSubTopics: Security challenges and their management in border areas – linkages of organized crime with terrorism
Various Security forces and agencies and their mandate
Cyber security
prelimsPoints: Joint cyber security exercises enhance international cooperation against cyber threats.
Critical infrastructure includes power grids, financial systems, transportation networks.
CERT-In (Indian Computer Emergency Response Team) is the national agency for cyber security incidents.
India-US strategic partnership extends to cyber domain.
Focus on sharing threat intelligence and coordinated response.
mainsPoints: Discuss the evolving nature of cyber threats and their implications for national security and economic stability.
Analyze the importance of international cooperation, such as 'Cyber Shield 2025', in building a resilient cyber defense.
Evaluate India's preparedness to counter sophisticated cyber-attacks on its critical infrastructure.
Examine the challenges in establishing a comprehensive cyber security framework, including legal and technological aspects.
Suggest policy measures for enhancing public-private partnerships in cyber security and fostering a culture of cyber hygiene.
articles.imageDescription: Binary code forming a shield icon, representing cyber security.
articles.trivia: The first computer virus, known as 'Creeper', was detected on ARPANET (predecessor to the internet) in the early 1970s.

articles.headline: Parliamentary Panel Recommends Reforms in Electoral Bonds Scheme to Enhance Transparency
articles.explainer:
• A parliamentary standing committee submitted its report recommending significant reforms to the Electoral Bonds Scheme.
• The committee suggested measures to enhance transparency, including disclosure of donor identities to the Election Commission of India (ECI).
• Concerns were raised regarding the anonymity of donors and its potential impact on electoral funding transparency and accountability.
• Recommendations also included setting a cap on corporate donations and strengthening the regulatory oversight of political funding.
• The report aims to address criticisms regarding the scheme's opacity and ensure fair electoral practices.
gsSubTopics: Salient features of the Representation of People’s Act
Appointment to various Constitutional posts, powers, functions and responsibilities of various Constitutional Bodies
Electoral reforms
prelimsPoints: Electoral Bonds Scheme introduced in 2017.
Allows individuals and corporates to donate to political parties anonymously.
Issued by State Bank of India (SBI) in denominations of Rs 1,000 to Rs 1 crore.
Valid for 15 days from the date of issue.

Only political parties registered under Section 29A of the Representation of the People Act, 1951, and having secured at least 1% of votes in the last general or assembly election, are eligible to receive bonds.
mainsPoints: Critically analyze the Electoral Bonds Scheme in terms of its stated objectives versus its impact on electoral transparency.
Discuss the arguments for and against donor anonymity in political funding.
Evaluate the role of the Election Commission of India (ECI) and parliamentary committees in advocating for electoral reforms.
Examine the implications of opaque political funding for democratic governance and accountability.
Suggest alternative mechanisms for political funding that promote transparency while encouraging legitimate donations.
articles.imageDescription: A ballot box with currency notes, symbolizing political funding and elections.
articles.trivia: The first general elections in independent India were held from October 1951 to February 1952.

articles.headline: Government Announces New Scheme to Boost MSME Exports and Global Competitiveness
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of MSME launched a new scheme, 'Udyam Niryat Protsahan Yojana', aimed at enhancing the export capabilities of Micro, Small, and Medium Enterprises.
• The scheme provides financial assistance for market research, participation in international trade fairs, and product certification.
• It also includes capacity building programs and mentorship to help MSMEs navigate international trade regulations and logistics.
• The initiative seeks to integrate Indian MSMEs into global value chains and increase their contribution to national exports.
• This move is expected to create new employment opportunities and strengthen India's position in global trade.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment
Effects of liberalization on the economy, changes in industrial policy and their effects on industrial growth
Inclusive growth and issues arising from it
prelimsPoints: MSMEs are defined based on investment in plant & machinery/equipment and turnover.
MSMEs contribute significantly to India's GDP, employment, and exports.
Udyam Registration Portal is for MSME classification.
The scheme provides financial and capacity building support.
Aims to enhance MSME presence in international markets.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of the MSME sector for India's economic growth, employment generation, and export performance.
Analyze the challenges faced by Indian MSMEs in accessing international markets and competing globally.
Evaluate the effectiveness of government schemes like 'Udyam Niryat Protsahan Yojana' in boosting MSME exports.
Examine how integration into global value chains can benefit Indian MSMEs.
Suggest policy measures to further support MSME growth, innovation, and global competitiveness.
articles.imageDescription: Small factory buildings with a world map in the background, symbolizing MSME global reach.
articles.trivia: The MSMED Act, 2006, provides the legal framework for the promotion, development, and enhancement of the competitiveness of MSMEs.

articles.headline: India-France Strategic Dialogue Discusses Indo-Pacific Cooperation and Defence Partnership
articles.explainer:
• India and France held their annual Strategic Dialogue, focusing on enhancing cooperation in the Indo-Pacific region and deepening their defense partnership.
• Discussions covered maritime security, counter-terrorism, and joint exercises, reflecting shared strategic interests.
• Both nations reiterated their commitment to a free, open, and inclusive Indo-Pacific, upholding international law.
• The dialogue also explored collaboration in critical technologies, space, and clean energy initiatives.
• This engagement reinforces the long-standing strategic autonomy and multi-polar world vision shared by India and France.
gsSubTopics: Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests
prelimsPoints: India and France established a Strategic Partnership in 1998.
France is a key partner in India's Indo-Pacific strategy.
Defence cooperation includes supply of Rafale jets and Scorpene submarines.
Both countries advocate for a rules-based international order.
Annual strategic dialogue covers various facets of bilateral ties.
mainsPoints: Analyze the evolution and significance of the India-France Strategic Partnership in the context of changing global geopolitics.
Discuss the convergence of interests between India and France in ensuring stability and security in the Indo-Pacific region.
Evaluate the role of defense cooperation in strengthening India's strategic capabilities and promoting 'Make in India' in defence.
Examine the potential for collaboration in critical and emerging technologies, space, and climate action.
Suggest measures to further deepen the bilateral relationship beyond traditional areas of cooperation.
articles.imageDescription: Flags of India and France intertwined, with a map of the Indo-Pacific in the background.
articles.trivia: France was the first P5 nation to establish a strategic partnership with India.

articles.headline: Urban Local Bodies to Adopt 'Blue-Green Infrastructure' for Climate Resilience
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Housing and Urban Affairs announced a new policy framework encouraging Urban Local Bodies (ULBs) to adopt 'Blue-Green Infrastructure' (BGI).
• BGI integrates natural elements like parks, green spaces, wetlands, and permeable surfaces into urban planning to manage water and mitigate climate impacts.
• The initiative aims to enhance urban biodiversity, improve air quality, reduce heat island effects, and promote sustainable water management.
• Pilot projects will be launched in several cities, focusing on stormwater management, urban cooling, and ecosystem restoration.
• This policy aligns with India's commitments to sustainable urbanization and climate change adaptation.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment
Disaster and disaster management
Urbanization, their problems and their remedies
articles.source: Ministry of Housing and Urban Affairs
prelimsPoints: BGI integrates natural systems into urban design.
Aims to address challenges like urban flooding, heat stress, and pollution.
Includes elements like urban forests, wetlands, rain gardens, green roofs.
Promotes sustainable water management and biodiversity conservation.
Part of climate change adaptation strategies for cities.
mainsPoints: Discuss the concept of Blue-Green Infrastructure and its benefits for sustainable urban development in India.
Analyze how BGI can effectively address challenges such as urban flooding, air pollution, and heat island effects.
Evaluate the role of Urban Local Bodies (ULBs) and citizen participation in the successful implementation of BGI projects.
Examine the financial and technical challenges in integrating BGI into existing urban landscapes.
Suggest policy interventions and incentives to promote the widespread adoption of BGI across Indian cities.
articles.imageDescription: An artistic rendering of a green city with rivers, parks, and green buildings.
articles.trivia: Singapore is a leading example of a 'City in a Garden' that has extensively adopted blue-green infrastructure principles.

prelimsSyllabus.subject: Environment
prelimsSyllabus.topic: Sustainable Urban Planning and Climate Adaptation

articles.headline: Breakthrough in Indigenous Vaccine Development for Neglected Tropical Diseases
articles.explainer:
• Indian scientists announced a significant breakthrough in developing a fully indigenous vaccine candidate for a cluster of Neglected Tropical Diseases (NTDs).
• The vaccine, developed by a consortium of public and private research institutions, showed promising results in preclinical trials.
• This innovation aims to address the high burden of NTDs in India and other developing countries, which disproportionately affect vulnerable populations.
• The government pledged accelerated funding and regulatory support for clinical trials and eventual mass production.
• This development highlights India's growing capabilities in biotechnology and pharmaceutical R&D, contributing to global health equity.
gsSubTopics: Science and Technology-developments and their applications and effects in everyday life
Achievements of Indians in science & technology; indigenization of technology and developing new technology
Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health
prelimsPoints: NTDs are a diverse group of communicable diseases prevalent in tropical and subtropical regions.
India has a significant burden of NTDs like Kala-azar, Lymphatic Filariasis, Dengue.
Indigenous vaccine development boosts self-reliance in healthcare.
Preclinical trials are conducted on animals before human trials.
Accelerated regulatory approvals are crucial for public health emergencies.
mainsPoints: Discuss the socio-economic burden of Neglected Tropical Diseases (NTDs) in India and the importance of indigenous vaccine development.
Analyze the role of public-private partnerships and government support in accelerating biopharmaceutical research and development.
Evaluate India's capabilities as a global pharmaceutical hub and its contribution to global health security.
Examine the ethical considerations and regulatory challenges in vaccine development and deployment.
Suggest strategies for ensuring equitable access to new vaccines, especially for vulnerable populations in low-income settings.
articles.imageDescription: Microscope and vaccine vials, symbolizing scientific research and medical breakthroughs.
articles.trivia: India has successfully eliminated several NTDs, including Yaws and Maternal and Neonatal Tetanus.

articles.headline: 'Poshan Abhiyaan' Enters New Phase with Focus on Adolescent Nutrition and Anaemia Control
articles.explainer:
• The flagship 'Poshan Abhiyaan' (National Nutrition Mission) launched its next phase, specifically targeting the nutritional needs of adolescents.
• The expanded focus aims to address the high prevalence of anaemia and malnutrition among adolescent girls and boys across the country.
• New interventions include targeted awareness campaigns, improved access to nutrient-rich foods, and health education in schools.
• The Ministry of Women and Child Development emphasized a multi-sectoral approach, involving health, education, and social welfare departments.
• This phase seeks to break the intergenerational cycle of malnutrition by investing in the health of future parents.
gsSubTopics: Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources
Welfare schemes for vulnerable sections of the population by the Centre and States and the performance of these schemes
articles.source: Ministry of Women and Child Development
prelimsPoints: Poshan Abhiyaan launched in 2018 to improve nutritional outcomes.
Aims to reduce stunting, undernutrition, anaemia, and low birth weight.
Uses technology and convergence to achieve targets.
Focus on 1000-day window from conception to child's second birthday.
New phase targets adolescents, especially girls, to address anaemia.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of focusing on adolescent nutrition for breaking the intergenerational cycle of malnutrition in India.
Analyze the causes and consequences of high prevalence of anaemia among adolescent girls in India.
Evaluate the effectiveness of 'Poshan Abhiyaan' in improving nutritional indicators across different age groups.
Examine the challenges in implementing nutrition programs, particularly in remote and marginalized communities.
Suggest a multi-sectoral approach and policy interventions to achieve comprehensive nutritional security for all age groups.
articles.imageDescription: A group of healthy adolescents engaged in sports, symbolizing good nutrition and well-being.
articles.trivia: India's National Food Security Act, 2013, aims to provide food and nutritional security by ensuring access to adequate quantity of quality food at affordable prices.

articles.headline: Archaeological Survey of India Unearths Ancient Buddhist Monastery in Odisha
articles.explainer:
• The Archaeological Survey of India (ASI) announced the discovery of a well-preserved ancient Buddhist monastery complex in Odisha's Jajpur district.
• The excavation revealed several stupas, statues of Buddha and Bodhisattvas, prayer halls, and residential cells, dating back to the Gupta period.
• This discovery sheds new light on the spread of Buddhism in eastern India and its cultural exchanges with Southeast Asia.
• Artifacts found at the site include pottery, terracotta seals, and inscriptions, providing valuable insights into the monastic life and art of the era.
• The site is expected to become a significant archaeological and tourist destination, promoting cultural heritage.
gsSubTopics: Indian culture will cover the salient aspects of Art Forms, Literature and Architecture from ancient to modern times
Archaeological sites
prelimsPoints: ASI is responsible for archaeological research and conservation of cultural heritage in India.
Odisha has a rich Buddhist heritage, with sites like Ratnagiri, Udayagiri, and Lalitgiri (Diamond Triangle).
Gupta period (c. 320 to 550 CE) is considered the 'Golden Age' of ancient India for art, science, and literature.
Stupas are dome-shaped structures containing Buddhist relics.
Bodhisattvas are enlightened beings who delay nirvana to help others.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of Buddhist monastic complexes in understanding the socio-cultural and religious landscape of ancient India.
Analyze how archaeological discoveries contribute to our knowledge of historical periods, such as the Gupta Age, and regional histories.
Evaluate the challenges and importance of preserving and promoting India's archaeological heritage.
Examine the role of the Archaeological Survey of India (ASI) in exploring and protecting historical sites.
Discuss the potential of such discoveries for cultural tourism and local economic development in regions like Odisha.
articles.imageDescription: Excavated ruins of an ancient Buddhist monastery with stupas and statues.
articles.trivia: The famous Nalanda Mahavihara in Bihar was one of the largest Buddhist monasteries and learning centers in ancient India.

articles.headline: India's Services PMI Reaches Multi-Month High Amidst Strong Demand and Business Optimism
articles.explainer:
• India's Services Purchasing Managers' Index (PMI) surged to a multi-month high in July, indicating robust growth in the services sector.
• The expansion was driven by strong domestic demand, new business gains, and a positive outlook for future activity.
• Employment in the services sector also registered an increase, pointing towards a healthy job market.
• Input cost inflation, however, remained a concern, although firms managed to pass on some of these costs to consumers.
• This sustained growth in services contributes significantly to India's overall economic resilience and GDP expansion.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment
Growth and development
Inflation
prelimsPoints: PMI is an indicator of economic health, based on monthly surveys of purchasing managers.
A PMI reading above 50 indicates expansion, while below 50 indicates contraction.
Services sector is a major contributor to India's GDP.
Factors influencing PMI include new orders, output, employment, and input prices.
S&P Global compiles India's PMI data.
articles.imageDescription: A bar graph showing an upward trend in economic indicators, symbolizing growth.
articles.trivia: The services sector accounted for over 50% of India's Gross Value Added (GVA) in recent years.

articles.headline: Centre Constitutes Expert Committee on Uniform Civil Code Implementation
articles.explainer:
• The Union Government constituted an expert committee to examine the implementation of a Uniform Civil Code (UCC) across India.
• The committee, comprising legal experts, constitutional scholars, and social scientists, will study existing personal laws and societal implications.
• Its mandate includes engaging with various religious and tribal communities to build consensus and address concerns.
• The move aligns with Article 44 of the Indian Constitution, which mandates the state to endeavor to secure a UCC for its citizens.
• The committee's recommendations are expected to pave the way for a more unified and equitable legal framework.
gsSubTopics: Indian Constitution—historical underpinnings, evolution, features, amendments, significant provisions and basic structure
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation
prelimsPoints: UCC is mentioned in Article 44 of the Indian Constitution.
It is a Directive Principle of State Policy (DPSP), hence non-justiciable.
Aims to replace diverse personal laws (marriage, divorce, inheritance) with a common set of laws.
Goa is the only Indian state with a Uniform Civil Code.
The Law Commission of India has previously examined the UCC.
mainsPoints: Discuss the constitutional mandate and historical context of the Uniform Civil Code (UCC) in India.
Analyze the arguments for and against the implementation of a UCC, focusing on issues of secularism, gender justice, and religious freedom.
Evaluate the challenges involved in drafting and implementing a UCC that respects diverse cultural and religious practices.
Examine the potential socio-political implications of a UCC on different communities, including tribal communities.
Suggest a roadmap for building consensus and ensuring a consultative process for UCC implementation.
articles.imageDescription: Scales of justice with symbols representing different religions, under the Indian Constitution.
articles.trivia: The Shah Bano case (1985) brought the debate on Uniform Civil Code to national prominence.

prelimsSyllabus.subject: Polity
prelimsSyllabus.topic: Directive Principles of State Policy and Uniform Civil Code

articles.headline: NITI Aayog Report Highlights Success of Digital Public Infrastructure in Rural Transformation
articles.explainer:
• A NITI Aayog report lauded the significant role of India's Digital Public Infrastructure (DPI) in driving rural transformation and financial inclusion.
• The report highlighted the widespread adoption of Aadhaar, UPI, and Jan Dhan accounts in empowering rural populations.
• Case studies demonstrated improved access to government services, direct benefit transfers, and digital payments, reducing leakages.
• Challenges remain in bridging the digital divide, ensuring digital literacy, and enhancing cybersecurity in rural areas.
• The report recommended further expansion of digital services and localized content to maximize DPI's impact on inclusive growth.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment
Inclusive growth and issues arising from it
Science and Technology-developments and their applications and effects in everyday life
prelimsPoints: DPI refers to shared digital systems that are foundational for public and private sector innovation.
Aadhaar provides a unique identity number.
UPI facilitates instant real-time payments.
PMJDY (Pradhan Mantri Jan Dhan Yojana) aims for financial inclusion.
NITI Aayog is the premier policy 'think tank' of the Government of India.
mainsPoints: Discuss the concept of Digital Public Infrastructure (DPI) and its transformative potential for India's rural economy and governance.

articles.headline: India-Pakistan Hold Bilateral Talks on Indus Water Treaty Amidst Hydropower Project Concerns
articles.explainer:
• Officials from India and Pakistan held bilateral talks under the provisions of the Indus Water Treaty (IWT) to address ongoing water-sharing issues.
• Discussions focused on India's proposed hydropower projects on the Chenab river, which Pakistan views as potentially impacting its water flow.
• Both sides reiterated their commitment to resolve disputes amicably through the Permanent Indus Commission mechanism.
• The talks aimed at clarifying technical aspects of the projects and ensuring adherence to the treaty's provisions.
• The IWT, brokered by the World Bank, is one of the most enduring water-sharing treaties globally.
gsSubTopics: Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests
India and its neighborhood- relations
prelimsPoints: IWT signed in 1960 between India and Pakistan, brokered by the World Bank.
Allocates Western Rivers (Indus, Jhelum, Chenab) to Pakistan and Eastern Rivers (Ravi, Beas, Sutlej) to India.
Allows India to use Western Rivers for non-consumptive purposes like hydropower generation.
Permanent Indus Commission (PIC) is the bilateral mechanism for treaty implementation.
Chenab is one of the Western Rivers.
mainsPoints: Analyze the historical context and significance of the Indus Water Treaty as a model for transboundary water sharing.
Discuss the challenges and disputes that have arisen under the IWT, particularly concerning India's hydropower projects.
Evaluate the role of the Permanent Indus Commission (PIC) in resolving water-related issues between India and Pakistan.
Examine the geopolitical implications of water sharing in the Indus Basin for India-Pakistan relations.
Suggest measures to ensure the long-term sustainability and effective implementation of the IWT amidst changing climate and hydrological patterns.
articles.imageDescription: A map of the Indus River system with national boundaries, symbolizing transboundary water management.
articles.trivia: The Indus Water Treaty is often cited as one of the most successful water treaties in the world, surviving multiple conflicts between India and Pakistan.

articles.headline: Government Expands 'Startup India Seed Fund Scheme' to Boost Early-Stage Innovation
articles.explainer:
• The Department for Promotion of Industry and Internal Trade (DPIIT) announced the expansion of the 'Startup India Seed Fund Scheme'.
• The enhanced scheme will provide increased financial assistance to early-stage startups for prototype development, market entry, and commercialization.
• It aims to address the critical funding gap faced by startups in their initial phases, especially in Tier 2 and Tier 3 cities.
• The expansion includes broader eligibility criteria and a streamlined application process to encourage innovation across diverse sectors.
• This initiative is poised to further fuel India's vibrant startup ecosystem and foster a culture of entrepreneurship.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment
Inclusive growth and issues arising from it
Science and Technology-developments and their applications and effects in everyday life
prelimsPoints: Startup India Seed Fund Scheme launched in 2021.
Aims to provide financial assistance to eligible startups for proof of concept, prototype development, product trials, market entry, and commercialization.
Implemented by DPIIT, Ministry of Commerce and Industry.
Funds are disbursed through eligible incubators.
Supports startups across various sectors.
mainsPoints: Discuss the role of seed funding and early-stage support in fostering a robust startup ecosystem in India.
Analyze the challenges faced by startups in Tier 2 and Tier 3 cities in accessing capital and mentorship.
Evaluate the impact of government initiatives like 'Startup India' on promoting entrepreneurship and job creation.
Examine how innovation in startups can drive economic diversification and technological advancement.
Suggest measures to further strengthen the funding landscape and regulatory environment for Indian startups.
articles.imageDescription: A stylized icon of a growing plant seedling emerging from a gear, symbolizing startup growth and innovation.
articles.trivia: India is home to the third-largest startup ecosystem in the world, after the US and China.

articles.headline: Government Finalizes National Data Governance Policy Framework for Non-Personal Data
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology (MeitY) announced the finalization of the National Data Governance Policy Framework.
• The framework aims to standardize data collection, storage, and access protocols for non-personal data, promoting responsible data sharing.
• It seeks to unlock the economic value of non-personal data for public good, innovation, and research while ensuring data privacy.
• Key provisions include the establishment of an India Data Management Office (IDMO) and guidelines for data anonymization and sharing.
• This policy is crucial for India's digital economy, enabling data-driven governance and fostering a data-rich ecosystem.

E-governance- applications, models, successes, limitations, and potential; citizens charters, transparency & accountability and institutional and other measures
Science and Technology-developments and their applications and effects in everyday life
articles.source: Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology
prelimsPoints: Non-personal data refers to data that does not relate to an identified or identifiable natural person.
MeitY is the nodal ministry for the framework.
Aims to create a robust data governance system for non-personal data.
IDMO (India Data Management Office) will be the central body.
Focus on responsible data sharing and unlocking economic value.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of a National Data Governance Policy Framework for non-personal data in India's digital economy.
Analyze the distinction between personal and non-personal data and the challenges in governing non-personal data effectively.
Evaluate the potential benefits of responsible data sharing for public good, innovation, and research.
Examine the role of the India Data Management Office (IDMO) in implementing and overseeing the policy framework.
Suggest measures to address concerns related to data privacy, data security, and potential monopolization of data resources under the new framework.
articles.imageDescription: Interconnected data points forming a network, symbolizing data governance and digital information flow.
articles.trivia: The concept of 'data as the new oil' highlights its immense economic value in the digital age.

prelimsMocks.question:
With reference to the National Green Hydrogen Mission, consider the following statements:
1. The mission aims to make India a global hub for the production, utilization, and export of Green Hydrogen.
2. The Strategic Interventions for Green Hydrogen Transition (SIGHT) programme is a key component of the mission.
3. The Ministry of Power is the nodal ministry responsible for its implementation.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statement 1 is correct. The mission's overarching objective is to position India as a global hub for green hydrogen. Statement 2 is correct. SIGHT is a major component providing financial incentives. Statement 3 is incorrect. The Ministry of New and Renewable Energy (MNRE) is the nodal ministry for the National Green Hydrogen Mission, not the Ministry of Power.

prelimsMocks.question:
Which of the following statements about the Digital Personal Data Protection Act (DPDPA), 2023, are correct?
1. It applies to the processing of digital personal data outside India if it is for offering goods or services to Data Principals in India.
2. It establishes the Data Protection Board of India as an independent body.
3. It introduces the concept of 'deemed consent' for certain legitimate uses of data.
Select the correct answer using the code given below:
prelimsMocks.explanation: All three statements are correct. The DPDPA has extra-territorial application (Statement 1). It establishes the Data Protection Board of India (Statement 2). It also includes provisions for 'deemed consent' in specific circumstances (Statement 3), which was a point of contention addressed by the Supreme Court.

prelimsMocks.question:
Consider the following countries:
1. Bangladesh
2. Bhutan
3. Nepal
4. Maldives
5. Sri Lanka
Which of the above are member countries of BIMSTEC?
prelimsMocks.explanation: BIMSTEC (Bay of Bengal Initiative for Multi-Sectoral Technical and Economic Cooperation) comprises seven member states: Bangladesh, Bhutan, India, Myanmar, Nepal, Sri Lanka, and Thailand. Maldives is not a member of BIMSTEC.

prelimsMocks.question: With reference to Project Tiger, which of the following statements is NOT correct?
options: (a) It was launched in 1973 for the conservation of tigers in India.
(b) The National Tiger Conservation Authority (NTCA) is the statutory body for its implementation.
(c) India is home to approximately 50% of the world's wild tiger population.
(d) M-STrIPES is a surveillance system used for monitoring tigers in reserves.
prelimsMocks.answer: (c) India is home to approximately 50% of the world's wild tiger population.
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statements (a), (b), and (d) are correct. Project Tiger was indeed launched in 1973, NTCA is the implementing body, and M-STrIPES is a key monitoring tool. Statement (c) is incorrect; India is home to over 70% of the world's wild tiger population, not 50%.

prelimsMocks.question: What is the primary objective of ISRO's Earth Observation Satellites (EOS) series?
options: (a) To conduct deep space exploration missions.
(b) To provide global positioning and navigation services.
(c) To collect data for applications in agriculture, forestry, disaster management, and urban planning.
(d) To facilitate satellite internet services across remote areas.
prelimsMocks.answer: (c) To collect data for applications in agriculture, forestry, disaster management, and urban planning.
prelimsMocks.explanation: Earth Observation Satellites (EOS) are specifically designed for remote sensing of the Earth's surface and atmosphere. Their primary objective is to collect valuable data for a wide range of applications, including resource management, environmental monitoring, and disaster mitigation, as mentioned in option (c). Options (a), (b), and (d) relate to other types of satellites or missions (e.g., interplanetary probes, navigation satellites, communication satellites).

GS1.headline: Archaeological Survey of India Unearths Ancient Buddhist Monastery in Odisha
GS1.explainer:
• The Archaeological Survey of India (ASI) announced the discovery of a well-preserved ancient Buddhist monastery complex in Odisha's Jajpur district.
• The excavation revealed several stupas, statues of Buddha and Bodhisattvas, prayer halls, and residential cells, dating back to the Gupta period.
• This discovery sheds new light on the spread of Buddhism in eastern India and its cultural exchanges with Southeast Asia.
• Artifacts found at the site include pottery, terracotta seals, and inscriptions, providing valuable insights into the monastic life and art of the era.
• The site is expected to become a significant archaeological and tourist destination, promoting cultural heritage.
gsSubTopics: Indian culture will cover the salient aspects of Art Forms, Literature and Architecture from ancient to modern times
Archaeological sites
prelimsPoints: ASI is responsible for archaeological research and conservation of cultural heritage in India.
Odisha has a rich Buddhist heritage, with sites like Ratnagiri, Udayagiri, and Lalitgiri (Diamond Triangle).
Gupta period (c. 320 to 550 CE) is considered the 'Golden Age' of ancient India for art, science, and literature.
Stupas are dome-shaped structures containing Buddhist relics.
Bodhisattvas are enlightened beings who delay nirvana to help others.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of Buddhist monastic complexes in understanding the socio-cultural and religious landscape of ancient India.
Analyze how archaeological discoveries contribute to our knowledge of historical periods, such as the Gupta Age, and regional histories.
Evaluate the challenges and importance of preserving and promoting India's archaeological heritage.
Examine the role of the Archaeological Survey of India (ASI) in exploring and protecting historical sites.
Discuss the potential of such discoveries for cultural tourism and local economic development in regions like Odisha.
GS1.imageDescription: Excavated ruins of an ancient Buddhist monastery with stupas and statues.
GS1.trivia: The famous Nalanda Mahavihara in Bihar was one of the largest Buddhist monasteries and learning centers in ancient India.

GS2.headline: Supreme Court Upholds Key Provisions of Digital Personal Data Protection Act, 2023
GS2.explainer:
• The Supreme Court, in a landmark judgment, largely upheld the constitutional validity of the Digital Personal Data Protection Act (DPDPA), 2023.
• The ruling addressed petitions challenging aspects related to data processing, consent mechanisms, and the powers of the Data Protection Board.
• The Court emphasized the balance between individual privacy rights and legitimate state interests, including national security and public order.
• Specific clarifications were issued regarding the 'deemed consent' provisions and the independence of the Data Protection Board.
• This judgment provides much-needed legal clarity for technology companies and data fiduciaries operating in India.
gsSubTopics: Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation
Structure, organization and functioning of the Executive and the Judiciary
Fundamental rights
prelimsPoints: DPDPA, 2023 replaced the Personal Data Protection Bill, 2019.
Key principles include consent, data minimization, accuracy, storage limitation.
Establishes the Data Protection Board of India.
Applies to processing of digital personal data within India and certain offshore processing.
Right to be forgotten and right to correction/erasure are part of the Act.
mainsPoints: Analyze how the DPDPA balances individual privacy rights with national security and economic interests.
Discuss the challenges in implementing the DPDPA, particularly for small and medium enterprises (SMEs).
Evaluate the role and independence of the Data Protection Board in safeguarding citizen's data.
Compare India's data protection framework with international standards like GDPR.
Examine the implications of the Supreme Court's judgment for the digital economy and innovation in India.
GS2.imageDescription: A gavel and a digital screen displaying code, symbolizing law and technology.
GS2.trivia: The 'Right to Privacy' was declared a fundamental right by the Supreme Court of India in the K.S. Puttaswamy judgment of 2017.

GS2.headline: India Hosts BIMSTEC Ministerial Meeting, Emphasizes Regional Connectivity and Trade Facilitation
GS2.explainer:
• India hosted the annual Ministerial Meeting of the Bay of Bengal Initiative for Multi-Sectoral Technical and Economic Cooperation (BIMSTEC).
• The agenda focused on accelerating regional connectivity projects, boosting intra-BIMSTEC trade, and strengthening cooperation in disaster management and climate resilience.
• Member states reaffirmed their commitment to the BIMSTEC Master Plan for Transport Connectivity, aiming to enhance physical and digital linkages.
• Discussions also covered food security, public health cooperation, and combating transnational crimes.
• The meeting concluded with a joint declaration emphasizing a 'prosperous, resilient, and peaceful Bay of Bengal region'.
gsSubTopics: Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests
prelimsPoints: BIMSTEC comprises 7 member states: Bangladesh, Bhutan, India, Myanmar, Nepal, Sri Lanka, and Thailand.
Formed in 1997 with the Bangkok Declaration.
Focuses on 14 priority sectors, including trade, technology, energy, transport, tourism, and fisheries.
Secretariat located in Dhaka, Bangladesh.
India considers BIMSTEC a key platform for its 'Act East' and 'Neighborhood First' policies.
mainsPoints: Analyze the strategic importance of BIMSTEC for India's regional foreign policy and economic interests.
Discuss the challenges hindering BIMSTEC's progress in achieving its objectives, particularly compared to SAARC.
Evaluate the potential of enhanced connectivity projects (physical and digital) within the BIMSTEC region.
Examine how BIMSTEC can contribute to addressing non-traditional security threats in the Bay of Bengal.
Suggest measures to strengthen BIMSTEC's institutional framework and enhance its effectiveness.
GS2.imageDescription: Flags of BIMSTEC member states arranged around a map of the Bay of Bengal region.
GS2.trivia: Four of the BIMSTEC member states (India, Bangladesh, Myanmar, Sri Lanka) are also members of SAARC.

GS2.headline: National Education Policy 2020 Implementation Review Highlights Progress in Skill Development
GS2.explainer:
• A comprehensive review of the National Education Policy (NEP) 2020 implementation noted significant progress, particularly in vocational education and skill development.
• The Ministry of Education highlighted increased integration of vocational courses from secondary levels and the establishment of skill hubs.
• Challenges remain in achieving universal foundational literacy and numeracy, especially in remote areas.
• The review emphasized the need for greater teacher training, digital infrastructure enhancement, and equitable access to quality education.
• Stakeholders called for accelerated reforms to fully realize NEP's vision of a holistic and flexible education system.
gsSubTopics: Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors
prelimsPoints: NEP 2020 replaced the National Policy on Education of 1986.
Aims for universalization of education from pre-school to secondary level.
Introduces 5+3+3+4 curricular structure.
Focuses on multidisciplinary education, vocational integration, and critical thinking.

mainsPoints: Analyze the key achievements and persistent challenges in the implementation of NEP 2020.
Discuss the role of vocational education and skill development in addressing youth unemployment and enhancing employability.
Evaluate the effectiveness of digital learning initiatives in bridging educational disparities, especially post-pandemic.
Examine the importance of teacher training and capacity building for successful educational reforms.
Suggest policy measures to ensure equitable access to quality education across all sections of society, as envisioned by NEP.
GS2.imageDescription: Students engaged in hands-on learning activities, symbolizing skill development and practical education.
GS2.trivia: The Kothari Commission (1964-66) was instrumental in shaping India's education policy, leading to the first National Policy on Education in 1968.

GS2.headline: Parliamentary Panel Recommends Reforms in Electoral Bonds Scheme to Enhance Transparency
GS2.explainer:
• A parliamentary standing committee submitted its report recommending significant reforms to the Electoral Bonds Scheme.
• The committee suggested measures to enhance transparency, including disclosure of donor identities to the Election Commission of India (ECI).
• Concerns were raised regarding the anonymity of donors and its potential impact on electoral funding transparency and accountability.
• Recommendations also included setting a cap on corporate donations and strengthening the regulatory oversight of political funding.
• The report aims to address criticisms regarding the scheme's opacity and ensure fair electoral practices.
gsSubTopics: Salient features of the Representation of People’s Act
Appointment to various Constitutional posts, powers, functions and responsibilities of various Constitutional Bodies
Electoral reforms
prelimsPoints: Electoral Bonds Scheme introduced in 2017.
Allows individuals and corporates to donate to political parties anonymously.
Issued by State Bank of India (SBI) in denominations of Rs 1,000 to Rs 1 crore.
Valid for 15 days from the date of issue.

Only political parties registered under Section 29A of the Representation of the People Act, 1951, and having secured at least 1% of votes in the last general or assembly election, are eligible to receive bonds.
mainsPoints: Critically analyze the Electoral Bonds Scheme in terms of its stated objectives versus its impact on electoral transparency.
Discuss the arguments for and against donor anonymity in political funding.
Evaluate the role of the Election Commission of India (ECI) and parliamentary committees in advocating for electoral reforms.
Examine the implications of opaque political funding for democratic governance and accountability.
Suggest alternative mechanisms for political funding that promote transparency while encouraging legitimate donations.
GS2.imageDescription: A ballot box with currency notes, symbolizing political funding and elections.
GS2.trivia: The first general elections in independent India were held from October 1951 to February 1952.

GS2.headline: India-France Strategic Dialogue Discusses Indo-Pacific Cooperation and Defence Partnership
GS2.explainer:
• India and France held their annual Strategic Dialogue, focusing on enhancing cooperation in the Indo-Pacific region and deepening their defense partnership.
• Discussions covered maritime security, counter-terrorism, and joint exercises, reflecting shared strategic interests.
• Both nations reiterated their commitment to a free, open, and inclusive Indo-Pacific, upholding international law.
• The dialogue also explored collaboration in critical technologies, space, and clean energy initiatives.
• This engagement reinforces the long-standing strategic autonomy and multi-polar world vision shared by India and France.

Defence cooperation includes supply of Rafale jets and Scorpene submarines.
Both countries advocate for a rules-based international order.
Annual strategic dialogue covers various facets of bilateral ties.
mainsPoints: Analyze the evolution and significance of the India-France Strategic Partnership in the context of changing global geopolitics.
Discuss the convergence of interests between India and France in ensuring stability and security in the Indo-Pacific region.
Evaluate the role of defense cooperation in strengthening India's strategic capabilities and promoting 'Make in India' in defence.

GS2.headline: 'Poshan Abhiyaan' Enters New Phase with Focus on Adolescent Nutrition and Anaemia Control
GS2.explainer:
• The flagship 'Poshan Abhiyaan' (National Nutrition Mission) launched its next phase, specifically targeting the nutritional needs of adolescents.
• The expanded focus aims to address the high prevalence of anaemia and malnutrition among adolescent girls and boys across the country.
• New interventions include targeted awareness campaigns, improved access to nutrient-rich foods, and health education in schools.
• The Ministry of Women and Child Development emphasized a multi-sectoral approach, involving health, education, and social welfare departments.
• This phase seeks to break the intergenerational cycle of malnutrition by investing in the health of future parents.

Welfare schemes for vulnerable sections of the population by the Centre and States and the performance of these schemes

Uses technology and convergence to achieve targets.
Focus on 1000-day window from conception to child's second birthday.
New phase targets adolescents, especially girls, to address anaemia.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of focusing on adolescent nutrition for breaking the intergenerational cycle of malnutrition in India.
Analyze the causes and consequences of high prevalence of anaemia among adolescent girls in India.
Evaluate the effectiveness of 'Poshan Abhiyaan' in improving nutritional indicators across different age groups.
Examine the challenges in implementing nutrition programs, particularly in remote and marginalized communities.
Suggest a multi-sectoral approach and policy interventions to achieve comprehensive nutritional security for all age groups.
GS2.imageDescription: A group of healthy adolescents engaged in sports, symbolizing good nutrition and well-being.
GS2.trivia: India's National Food Security Act, 2013, aims to provide food and nutritional security by ensuring access to adequate quantity of quality food at affordable prices.

GS2.headline: Centre Constitutes Expert Committee on Uniform Civil Code Implementation
GS2.explainer:
• The Union Government constituted an expert committee to examine the implementation of a Uniform Civil Code (UCC) across India.
• The committee, comprising legal experts, constitutional scholars, and social scientists, will study existing personal laws and societal implications.
• Its mandate includes engaging with various religious and tribal communities to build consensus and address concerns.
• The move aligns with Article 44 of the Indian Constitution, which mandates the state to endeavor to secure a UCC for its citizens.
• The committee's recommendations are expected to pave the way for a more unified and equitable legal framework.

It is a Directive Principle of State Policy (DPSP), hence non-justiciable.
Aims to replace diverse personal laws (marriage, divorce, inheritance) with a common set of laws.
Goa is the only Indian state with a Uniform Civil Code.
The Law Commission of India has previously examined the UCC.

Suggest a roadmap for building consensus and ensuring a consultative process for UCC implementation.

GS3.headline: India Unveils Phase II of National Green Hydrogen Mission, Targets Enhanced Production and Export
GS3.explainer:
• The Union Cabinet approved the second phase of the National Green Hydrogen Mission, aiming to significantly scale up indigenous production capacity.
• Phase II focuses on developing green hydrogen export hubs, establishing a robust supply chain, and attracting global investments.
• Key incentives include Production Linked Incentives (PLI) for electrolyser manufacturing and green hydrogen production, along with R&D support.
• The mission targets a substantial reduction in fossil fuel imports and an acceleration towards India's net-zero emissions goal by 2070.
• Emphasis is placed on creating a skilled workforce and fostering innovation in green hydrogen technologies.

GS3.headline: RBI Releases Biannual Financial Stability Report, Cautions on Global Headwinds
GS3.explainer:
• The Reserve Bank of India (RBI) released its latest Financial Stability Report (FSR), assessing the health of India's financial system.
• The report noted resilience in the banking sector, with improved asset quality and capital adequacy ratios.
• However, it cautioned about potential risks stemming from global economic slowdown, geopolitical tensions, and persistent inflation pressures.
• Non-banking financial companies (NBFCs) also showed signs of stability, though specific segments require close monitoring.
• The RBI emphasized the need for continued vigilance and proactive policy measures to mitigate emerging risks.

GS3.headline: Project Tiger Completes 52 Years: Successes, Challenges, and Future Roadmap for Conservation
GS3.explainer:
• Project Tiger, India's flagship tiger conservation program, marked its 52nd anniversary, celebrating significant strides in increasing tiger populations.
• A comprehensive review highlighted the expansion of protected areas, anti-poaching efforts, and community involvement as key successes.
• Challenges persist, including human-wildlife conflict, habitat fragmentation, and the impact of climate change on tiger corridors.
• The Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change outlined a future roadmap focusing on genetic diversity, smart patrolling, and landscape-level conservation.
• Emphasis was laid on integrating local communities into conservation strategies through livelihood support and awareness programs.

GS3.headline: ISRO Successfully Launches Advanced Earth Observation Satellite 'EOS-07'
GS3.explainer:
• The Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) successfully launched the Earth Observation Satellite-07 (EOS-07) from Satish Dhawan Space Centre.
• EOS-07 is an advanced remote sensing satellite designed for high-resolution imaging and data collection for various applications.
• Its payloads include enhanced optical sensors and synthetic aperture radar (SAR) for all-weather, all-time imaging capabilities.
• The satellite will primarily support applications in agriculture, forestry, disaster management, urban planning, and oceanography.
• This launch further strengthens India's indigenous capabilities in space-based earth observation and data analytics.

GS3.headline: India-US Joint Cyber Security Exercise 'Cyber Shield 2025' Concludes
GS3.explainer:
• The joint India-US cyber security exercise, 'Cyber Shield 2025', successfully concluded, enhancing cooperation in combating cyber threats.
• The exercise simulated complex cyber-attack scenarios targeting critical infrastructure and government networks.
• Participants from both nations shared best practices in threat intelligence, incident response, and forensic analysis.
• The drill aimed to strengthen interoperability and build a coordinated response mechanism against state-sponsored and criminal cyber activities.
• This collaboration underscores the growing strategic partnership between India and the US in the digital domain.

GS3.headline: Government Announces New Scheme to Boost MSME Exports and Global Competitiveness
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of MSME launched a new scheme, 'Udyam Niryat Protsahan Yojana', aimed at enhancing the export capabilities of Micro, Small, and Medium Enterprises.
• The scheme provides financial assistance for market research, participation in international trade fairs, and product certification.
• It also includes capacity building programs and mentorship to help MSMEs navigate international trade regulations and logistics.
• The initiative seeks to integrate Indian MSMEs into global value chains and increase their contribution to national exports.
• This move is expected to create new employment opportunities and strengthen India's position in global trade.

GS3.headline: Urban Local Bodies to Adopt 'Blue-Green Infrastructure' for Climate Resilience
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of Housing and Urban Affairs announced a new policy framework encouraging Urban Local Bodies (ULBs) to adopt 'Blue-Green Infrastructure' (BGI).
• BGI integrates natural elements like parks, green spaces, wetlands, and permeable surfaces into urban planning to manage water and mitigate climate impacts.
• The initiative aims to enhance urban biodiversity, improve air quality, reduce heat island effects, and promote sustainable water management.
• Pilot projects will be launched in several cities, focusing on stormwater management, urban cooling, and ecosystem restoration.
• This policy aligns with India's commitments to sustainable urbanization and climate change adaptation.

GS3.headline: Breakthrough in Indigenous Vaccine Development for Neglected Tropical Diseases
GS3.explainer:
• Indian scientists announced a significant breakthrough in developing a fully indigenous vaccine candidate for a cluster of Neglected Tropical Diseases (NTDs).
• The vaccine, developed by a consortium of public and private research institutions, showed promising results in preclinical trials.
• This innovation aims to address the high burden of NTDs in India and other developing countries, which disproportionately affect vulnerable populations.
• The government pledged accelerated funding and regulatory support for clinical trials and eventual mass production.
• This development highlights India's growing capabilities in biotechnology and pharmaceutical R&D, contributing to global health equity.

GS3.headline: India's Services PMI Reaches Multi-Month High Amidst Strong Demand and Business Optimism
GS3.explainer:
• India's Services Purchasing Managers' Index (PMI) surged to a multi-month high in July, indicating robust growth in the services sector.
• The expansion was driven by strong domestic demand, new business gains, and a positive outlook for future activity.
• Employment in the services sector also registered an increase, pointing towards a healthy job market.
• Input cost inflation, however, remained a concern, although firms managed to pass on some of these costs to consumers.
• This sustained growth in services contributes significantly to India's overall economic resilience and GDP expansion.

GS3.headline: NITI Aayog Report Highlights Success of Digital Public Infrastructure in Rural Transformation
GS3.explainer:
• A NITI Aayog report lauded the significant role of India's Digital Public Infrastructure (DPI) in driving rural transformation and financial inclusion.
• The report highlighted the widespread adoption of Aadhaar, UPI, and Jan Dhan accounts in empowering rural populations.
• Case studies demonstrated improved access to government services, direct benefit transfers, and digital payments, reducing leakages.
• Challenges remain in bridging the digital divide, ensuring digital literacy, and enhancing cybersecurity in rural areas.
• The report recommended further expansion of digital services and localized content to maximize DPI's impact on inclusive growth.

GS3.headline: Government Expands 'Startup India Seed Fund Scheme' to Boost Early-Stage Innovation
GS3.explainer:
• The Department for Promotion of Industry and Internal Trade (DPIIT) announced the expansion of the 'Startup India Seed Fund Scheme'.
• The enhanced scheme will provide increased financial assistance to early-stage startups for prototype development, market entry, and commercialization.
• It aims to address the critical funding gap faced by startups in their initial phases, especially in Tier 2 and Tier 3 cities.
• The expansion includes broader eligibility criteria and a streamlined application process to encourage innovation across diverse sectors.
• This initiative is poised to further fuel India's vibrant startup ecosystem and foster a culture of entrepreneurship.
